name: Lotte Lenya
abstract: Lotte Lenya (born Karoline Wilhelmine Charlotte Blamauer; 18 October 1898 – 27 November 1981) was an Austrian-American singer, diseuse, and actress, long based in the United States. In the German-speaking and classical music world, she is best remembered for her performances of the songs of her first husband, Kurt Weill.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'Come.' We went. She was sitting, and I approached from the side... I walked up. He just said, 'This is Annie Ross' and left. I said, 'Miss Lenya.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] It might have been Lotte Lenya (PHONETIC). Did you meet her during those years? [INTERVIEWEE] At that time, see, those girls were busy up there and I was in the band, I was busy, too. ... And so then I made this contract and I went back.
